steps.title: Use vertex form and determine the sign of a
steps.content: With vertex $(8, -18)$ the parabola is $y = a(x - 8)^2 - 18$. Since it crosses the x-axis at two points and the vertex is below the x-axis, the parabola opens upward, so $a \gt 0$.

steps.title: Express $a + b + c$ using $f(1)$
steps.content:
In $y = ax^2 + bx + c$, plugging $x = 1$ gives $f(1) = a + b + c$. Using vertex form:
$$a + b + c = a(1 - 8)^2 - 18 = 49a - 18$$

steps.title: Apply the constraint $a \gt 0$
steps.content: Since $a \gt 0$, we need $49a - 18 \gt -18$, so $a + b + c \gt -18$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $-27$: less than $-18$ — impossible, eliminate.
  • $-22$: less than $-18$ — impossible, eliminate.
  • $-18$: would require $a = 0$, not a parabola — eliminate.
  • $-16$: gives $49a - 18 = -16$, so $a = \frac{2}{49} \gt 0$ — works.
That confirms D is correct.
